mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress consumer spending expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth that lifts corporate earnings forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, which supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during low inflation periods yields negative 20-day forward returns in >50% of cases, rejecting the supply-side valuation support claim.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which consistently generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns over a 5-year sample, with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 2.5 percent triggers a liquidity contraction that raises borrowing costs for corporations and forces equity portfolio rebalancing, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5 percent, invalidating the liquidity contraction mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures signals weakening household demand relative to overall output, which compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence metric produces a Rank IC above 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows, contradicting the negative return prediction.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates that higher interest rates are successfully cooling economic activity, which reduces inflation expectations and triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns over one week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during tightening periods fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.96, rejecting the disinflation trade mechanism.
